§6Americium-242m§f

§aRare fissile americium isotope — highly reactive RBMK and PWR fuel!§f

§bAmericium-242m§f (specifically Am-242m1) is a rare fissile radioisotope used in advanced reactor fuels. It's produced only by the decay of §bPu-241§f in RBMK reactors.

§6Properties§f:
§b• Radioactivity§f: §e9.5§f RAD/s
§b• Category§f: Fissile
§b• Difficulty§f: Hard

§6Production§f (SILEX):
§b• Short-Lived Nuclear Waste§f + BiSmUTh crystal → yields Am-242 alongside Am-241
§b• Reactor Grade Americium§f → §e66%§f Am-242, §e33%§f Am-241

§6Uses§f:
§b• RBMK Fuel (HEA-242)§f: §e8§f Am-242 billets → HEA-242 RBMK fuel rod (shapeless)
§b• PWR Fuel (HEA-242)§f: Am-242 billet + Insulator + Am-242 billet → PWR fuel rod
§b• Reactor Grade Americium§f: combine with Am-241

§eTip§f: Am-242m is the fissile powerhouse of the americium isotopes. Both Am-241 and Am-242m come from the same SILEX process — you'll get both simultaneously when processing Pu-241 waste!
